page: Amazon Web Services c8i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with the Geekbench single-core score and single-threaded gzip compression both ranking in the top tier. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression also shows strong performance. However, multi-core scaling is more limited, as evidenced by weak ratings in multi-core stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmarks. Memory performance is mixed; while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths perform at an average level, cached small-block read bandwidth and the overall PassMark memory score fall into the lower tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.xlarge offers a dense compute profile with high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced CPU instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2 GB per core and the lack of local storage, which limits its utility for memory-intensive or disk-heavy applications. This instance is best suited for compute-bound workloads that benefit from high single-core speeds and vector instructions, such as lightweight web serving, single-threaded data compression, software compilation, and entry-level machine learning inference using small models.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight exceptional single-core capabilities, with top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core tests, though multi-core scaling and cached memory bandwidth are relatively weak. With advanced instruction support like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, the c8i.xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy, single-threaded tasks. It is highly suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, text processing, and lightweight model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 4 dedicated vCPUs, and 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and strong single-core performance designed for processing-intensive workloads and lightweight inference.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
8.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and text processing benchmarks
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
No local storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight inference
